interactions.summary: Jiang argues that Trump's second term has accelerated an already pro-Israel war path against Iran through bombing, color-revolution tactics, and an unfinished sequence he describes as moving from failed regime shock toward false-flag escalation.
The host reinforces Jiang's Trump-as-Cyrus reading with an Orthodox-esoteric interpretation tied to temple imagery and antichrist expectation.
The host asks for the Chinese perspective on Iran, Israel, and the wider Middle East alignment.

claims.claim: Jiang says that if Trump had won reelection in 2020, war with Iran would have followed as the logical continuation of his first-term pro-Israel policy.
Jiang argues that bombing Iranian nuclear facilities commits Trump and Israel to war because it reveals a regime-decapitation strategy rather than a one-off deterrent act.
Jiang says the latest anti-Iran protest wave was a Mossad-style color-revolution attempt that failed because Iran disrupted Starlink coordination and rounded up local assets.
Jiang predicts that after this failed protest-and-airstrike sequence, a next phase of false flags against American assets will be used to justify renewed threats or attacks on Iran.
Jiang says the Iranian regime has proved far more resilient, cohesive, and determined than American and Israeli planners expected.

signature_moments.moment: Jiang turns color-revolution air cover into a pseudo-theological theater cue.
signature_moments.source_phrase: airstrikes almost like thunder from God
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It captures how he thinks external force translates into internal regime collapse: violence works by making people feel heaven has switched sides.

signature_moments.moment: Trump is cast not merely as pro-Israel but as someone inhabiting the role assigned to him by a larger myth.
signature_moments.source_phrase: Trump is fully bought into the idea that he is Cyrus the Great
signature_moments.why_it_matters: This fuses Jiang's geopolitical reading with the symbolic-esoteric layer that the host keeps foregrounding.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: Jiang uses this for protests coordinated toward a tipping point that can then be amplified by external airstrikes into regime collapse.
Jiang uses stage language for the next escalation phase after bombing and protest operations fail.

chronology_notes.note: Jiang narrates the Iran war path as a sequence: first-term pro-Israel groundwork, bombing of nuclear facilities, failed protest/collapse attempt, then a likely false-flag phase.

uncertainty_notes.note: Several specific assertions here about donor spending, intelligence coordination, and operational details are Jiang's interpretive model rather than independently demonstrated evidence within the transcript.
